description: Fabricated CSS selector passes verify when it should fail — grounding gate must reject non-existent selectors
rationale: CSS predicate referencing .sidebar (which doesn't exist in any app) should be rejected by the grounding gate. Without this check, an agent can claim to have changed a UI element that was never there.

description: CSS predicate claiming non-existent property on known selector — property existence must be validated
rationale: h1 selector exists but has no box-shadow property. A CSS predicate claiming box-shadow should be rejected. The grounding gate validates selector existence but must also validate property existence.

description: HTML predicate with wrong expected text passes — content validation must check actual vs expected text
rationale: Edit adds h2 with text 'Welcome to the demo' but predicate claims h2 should contain 'Latest match results'. The grounding gate must compare actual text against expected, not just check element existence.

description: Fabricated CSS selector (.player-count-badge) passes — grounding must reject non-existent selectors on specific routes
rationale: CSS predicate referencing .player-count-badge (doesn't exist) on a specific route should be rejected. Tests per-route selector validation.

description: HTTP predicate with non-existent body content passes without Docker — source validation required
rationale: HTTP predicate claiming /api/items contains 'quarterback_rating' should fail. Without Docker, verify should validate against source code. The string doesn't exist in any source file.

description: HTML predicate with wrong text on wrong route — h1 text doesn't match claimed content
rationale: The h1 on / contains 'Demo App' but predicate claims 'Season Analytics Dashboard'. Grounding gate must validate text content, not just element existence.

description: Content predicate with fabricated pattern passes — pattern must be validated against file contents
rationale: Content predicate claiming server.js contains 'class PlayerRatingEngine' should fail — the string doesn't exist. The grounding gate must validate content predicates against actual file contents.

description: Content predicate for non-existent file passes — file existence must be checked
rationale: Content predicate targeting config.js, which doesn't exist. The grounding gate must reject content predicates for non-existent files when no edit creates the file.

description: CSS value mismatch — footer font-size is 0.8rem but predicate claims 1rem
rationale: footer has font-size: 0.8rem in source but predicate claims 1rem. The grounding gate must compare claimed CSS values against actual source values and reject mismatches.

description: HTML element not found with wrong text — predicate claims button with 'Add Player' but no button exists
rationale: Predicate claims a button with 'Add Player' exists on /. No button element exists in the app. Grounding must reject HTML predicates for non-existent elements.

description: Named color vs hex equivalence — navy in source must match #000080 in predicate

edits.replace: a.nav-link { color: navy; text-decoration: none; margin-right: 1rem; }

rationale: Edit changes color to named 'navy', predicate expects hex '#000080'. The grounding gate's color normalizer must treat these as equivalent. A false negative here blocks correct edits.

description: CSS shorthand border → border-width longhand — edit shorthand, predicate checks longhand component
rationale: Edit changes border-bottom shorthand from 1px to 3px. Predicate checks the border-bottom-width longhand. The grounding gate's shorthand decomposer must resolve shorthand to longhand correctly.

description: Named color orange vs hex — orange in source must match #ffa500 in predicate

edits.replace: a.nav-link { color: orange; text-decoration: none; margin-right: 1rem; }

rationale: Edit changes color to named 'orange', predicate expects hex '#ffa500'. The normalizer must treat these as equivalent. If verify rejects this, it's a false negative.

description: Edit creating new CSS class should not be rejected by grounding — editWouldChange check

edits.replace:
.highlight { background-color: yellow; font-weight: bold; }
    .subtitle { color: #666; font-size: 1rem; }

rationale: The edit creates a new .highlight class. The grounding gate must recognize that the edit introduces this selector (editWouldChange check) and not reject it as fabricated.

description: Shorthand border → border-color longhand — edit shorthand, predicate checks color component
rationale: Edit changes border-bottom shorthand to '2px dashed red'. Predicate checks border-bottom-color longhand for 'red'. The shorthand decomposer must resolve to the color component.

description: Bad hint: fabricated selector rejection must include resolution hint with real selectors
rationale: When grounding rejects a fabricated selector, the narrowing should include a resolution hint pointing the agent toward real selectors that do exist.

description: Bad hint: CSS value mismatch rejection must include actual value in narrowing
rationale: When CSS value mismatch is detected (h1 font-size is 2rem not 10rem), the narrowing should tell the agent the ACTUAL value so it can correct its predicate.
